mj-text-bKWuFGBO.content:
Hi {{contact.first_name}},

Summer might be heating up, but so is the real estate market—and not just in the usual ways.

This month brings something rare: a policy change that can actually put money back in your pocket, plus a wave of buyers and sellers trying to make sense of higher rates, stalled inventory, and shifting opportunity.

At Olive Tree Investments, we’re here to help you invest with clarity, not chaos. Whether you’re managing a multifamily portfolio, considering your first single-family buy, or exploring your next move, this month’s insights are designed to help you:

  • Decode the latest tax advantages

  • Navigate today’s lending environment with confidence

  • And make smart, time-sensitive decisions in a complex market

Let’s get into it.

In This Issue:

🧾 Bonus Depreciation Returns
What the new tax bill means for your next investment—and your last one.

🏠 Getting a Mortgage in 2025
Lending tips for buyers navigating today’s high-rate landscape.

💰 July 2025 Interest Rate Snapshot
The latest multifamily and residential rates—and what to do with them.

mj-text-bKWuFGBO-piT1bAyZ-XkxBz27A.content: → Why this tax change might be the most profitable update of the year

mj-text-zUU7TNhr.content:
The new House tax bill has revived 100% bonus depreciation, retroactive to January 1, 2023, and extended through 2025. If you own or plan to acquire capital-heavy properties, this changes everything.

Here’s why this matters:

✅ You can now deduct the entire cost of qualifying assets—from HVAC systems to parking lot improvements—in the year they’re placed in service.
✅ Cost segregation studies just became dramatically more valuable. Breaking out short-lived assets in your property can unlock six- or seven-figure deductions.
✅ It’s retroactive, so if you made qualifying investments in 2023, you may be able to amend your return and get a refund.

But there’s a deadline. The 100% rate drops again in 2026, and unless extended, this opportunity phases out completely by 2027.

Investor takeaway:
Use this window wisely. Review your 2023 acquisitions, plan upcoming projects, and talk to your CPA or tax strategist about maximizing accelerated depreciation while it lasts.

mj-text-iwAeSLUN.content: Getting a Mortgage in 2025

mj-text-TwppHz2a.content: → What single-family buyers need to know about lending this summer

mj-text-C6AJmHxe.content:
Yes, interest rates are still elevated—but that’s not the whole story. If you're thinking about buying, refinancing, or holding off, here's what really matters right now:

What lenders are looking for:

  • DTI (Debt-to-Income): Most lenders want it under 43%.

  • Credit Score: Aim for 740+ to access top-tier terms.

  • Documentation: Self-employed or investor? Prepare two years of returns, bank statements, and a clear financial picture.

What smart buyers are doing:

  • Exploring ARM options: A 5/1 ARM may offer lower upfront rates if you plan to refinance later.

  • Negotiating closing costs or rate buydowns with motivated sellers.

  • Locking based on affordability, not fear. Waiting for rates to drop isn’t always a winning bet—especially if prices rise in the meantime.

Bottom line:
Don’t let rates alone make your decision. If the home fits, and the payment makes sense, your future self will thank you.

mj-text-DKUspLNS.content: 💰 July 2025 Interest Rate Snapshot

mj-text-dcG0v2OP.content:
Here’s where financing stands this month for both multifamily and single-family deals.

Rates sourced from Mortgage News Daily, Apartment Loan Store, Select Commercial. Accurate as of July 2025.

mj-text-Lvtk2ipD.content: Key Takeaway: Market Wisdom for July 2025

mj-text-sr3BwGDZ.content:
Markets rarely move in straight lines—and neither should your investment strategy.

With 100% bonus depreciation back on the table, interest rates still reshaping buying power, and buyer sentiment hovering in wait-and-see mode, this month is all about timing your moves with precision.

Whether you’re:

  • Repositioning a value-add deal

  • Planning a sale in a high-rate environment

  • Or entering the single-family market with caution

Now’s the time to pair strategy with speed. Don’t wait for the “perfect” signal. The best investors move with informed confidence, not hesitation.

mj-text-eY3Yqusd.content: Let’s Build Something Real

mj-text-3rQjpfCg.content:
At Olive Tree Investments, we created this firm to simplify real estate investing—without dumbing it down. Whether you're investing $25K into your first syndication or overseeing a family office portfolio, our mission is the same:

✅ Help you find the right assets
✅ Improve their performance
✅ Buy or sell at the right time
✅ Grow your portfolio with purpose

Have questions about your next move? We’re here.

Brian Norton
Principal, Olive Tree Investments

📍 269 Market Place Blvd Suite 126, Cartersville, GA 30121

Bonus Depreciation Returns
→ Why this tax change might be the most profitable update of the year

The new House tax bill has revived 100% bonus depreciation, retroactive to January 1, 2023, and extended through 2025. If you own or plan to acquire capital-heavy properties, this changes everything.

Here’s why this matters:

✅ You can now deduct the entire cost of qualifying assets—from HVAC systems to parking lot improvements—in the year they’re placed in service.
✅ Cost segregation studies just became dramatically more valuable. Breaking out short-lived assets in your property can unlock six- or seven-figure deductions.
✅ It’s retroactive, so if you made qualifying investments in 2023, you may be able to amend your return and get a refund.

But there’s a deadline. The 100% rate drops again in 2026, and unless extended, this opportunity phases out completely by 2027.

Investor takeaway:
Use this window wisely. Review your 2023 acquisitions, plan upcoming projects, and talk to your CPA or tax strategist about maximizing accelerated depreciation while it lasts.
Getting a Mortgage in 2025
→ What single-family buyers need to know about lending this summer

Yes, interest rates are still elevated—but that’s not the whole story. If you're thinking about buying, refinancing, or holding off, here's what really matters right now:

What lenders are looking for:

  • DTI (Debt-to-Income): Most lenders want it under 43%.

  • Credit Score: Aim for 740+ to access top-tier terms.

  • Documentation: Self-employed or investor? Prepare two years of returns, bank statements, and a clear financial picture.

What smart buyers are doing:

  • Exploring ARM options: A 5/1 ARM may offer lower upfront rates if you plan to refinance later.

  • Negotiating closing costs or rate buydowns with motivated sellers.

  • Locking based on affordability, not fear. Waiting for rates to drop isn’t always a winning bet—especially if prices rise in the meantime.

Bottom line:
Don’t let rates alone make your decision. If the home fits, and the payment makes sense, your future self will thank you.
